author: StevenRoosa
text: Version 7.0.2 of the binary ships with and/or creates expired root cert on first install.  Event log: "warn: The mitmproxy certificate authority has expired"

author: StevenRoosa
text:
#### Problem Description

A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

The mitmproxy certificate authority ships with, or creates on first install, an expired root cert.

#### Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Download Version 7.0.2 of the binary.
2. Start mitmproxy.
3. Examine the Eventlog.

#### System Information

Paste the output of "mitmproxy --version" here.  
Mitmproxy: 7.0.2 binary  
Python: 3.9.5  
OpenSSL: OpenSSL 1.1.1k 25 Mar 2021  
Platform: Linux-5.4.0-84-generic-x86\_64-with-glibc2.31

author: Prinzhorn
text:
Did you by any chance had mitmproxy installed on that machine before (maybe years ago)? Then there might still be an old CA lingering in `~/.mitmproxy`. Or maybe some backup restored `~/.mitmproxy`, who knows.

The warning should contain instructions on what to do.

[mitmproxy/mitmproxy/addons/tlsconfig.py](https://github.com/mitmproxy/mitmproxy/blob/c90aaf55d96dc3f7722843b27fc9eb1034df3ac5/mitmproxy/addons/tlsconfig.py#L250-L256)

Lines 250 to 256
in
[c90aaf5](/mitmproxy/mitmproxy/commit/c90aaf55d96dc3f7722843b27fc9eb1034df3ac5)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | if self.certstore.default\_ca.has\_expired(): |
|  | ctx.log.warn( |
|  | "The mitmproxy certificate authority has expired!\n" |
|  | "Please delete all CA-related files in your ~/.mitmproxy folder.\n" |
|  | "The CA will be regenerated automatically after restarting mitmproxy.\n" |
|  | "See https://docs.mitmproxy.org/stable/concepts-certificates/ for additional help.", |
|  | ) |

Or does that happen on a clean machine?

author: StevenRoosa
text: You are absolutely right. I so embarrassed! Apologies for the distraction on this one....
